messages.content: how's iris doing
Got into NYU. Hasn't told her mother yet.
how's cole doing
His pages got denser. The chapter about his uncle is the strongest thing he's written.
how's mac doing
Came back. Brought a friend this time.
how's reed doing
Reed eats the snacks. Sat through a whole reading wednesday. First time.
how's alma doing
Painting again. Bigger canvases. Quiet about it.
how's jordan now
Brought a poem about his sister. Read it out loud.
who came in today
Iris. Cole. Mac and a new one named Theo. Reed at four for snacks.
how was the saturday workshop
Good. Cole read first. Iris cried, didn't try to hide it. We sat with it.
who's the new one
Devon. Sixteen. Cole brought him.
is mac still coming
Twice a week. Sits at the back. Hasn't read out loud yet.
how's iris's mother situation
Tense. Iris is staying at her aunt's till the NYU thing settles.
how's the new kid devon
Quiet. Watches. Hasn't put pen to paper yet but he's listening.
did jordan come back
Saturday. Brought a poem. The strongest he's done.
is reed still just here for snacks
Mostly. He listened today. That's a shift.
how's cole's home situation
Stable for now. His mom's working two jobs. He's holding.
what's iris working on
A long essay about her grandmother's funeral. The middle is the strongest.
any new kids this week
Two. Devon, sixteen. A girl named Kit, fourteen. Both quiet so far.
how's mac's writing
Fragments. The fragments are the strongest part. Don't push him to finish anything yet.
did alma show new work
Three small canvases on the back wall. The middle one stops you.
how's cole handling the recommendation letter for nyu
Anxious. I told him I'd have it by friday.
is iris talking to her mom yet
Not yet. Her aunt is acting as the buffer.
who read out loud this week
Iris. Cole. Devon, two lines. That was a thing.
is jordan still eating dinner with you
Tuesdays, when Lou's here. Naomi sometimes joins.
what did the kids work on today
Free-write for the first half. The second half we read each other's. Cole and Iris exchanged.
is reed going to write something
Eventually. He's still figuring out if he's allowed to.
is the new girl writing
Kit. She brought a notebook she never opens. We're letting her be.
how's the workshop attendance
Steady. Eight regular. Two new in the past month.
is cole still anxious about nyu
Less. The portfolio went out monday. Now we wait.
any drama with the kids this week
Iris and Mac had words about whose pages went first. Worked it out.
who's been struggling lately
Mac. Sister moved out. He's quieter than usual.
what did jordan write about this time
His grandfather's hands. The poem that landed for everyone in the room.
how's iris's college search going
NYU acceptance. UT waitlist. Sarah Lawrence in two weeks.
who showed up unexpectedly
Devon. He was supposed to be sick. Stayed for both halves.
is alma still in the corner
By the window now. She moved her easel last tuesday. Means something.
who needs more attention right now
Mac. He's holding too much. I'm watching.
any breakthroughs this week
Iris finished the essay. Cole started a new chapter. Devon read out loud. Three things.
are the kids writing about hard stuff
Mostly yes. Cole about his uncle. Iris her grandmother. Mac about his sister.
who didn't show up this week
Reed. Didn't text. I'll check tomorrow.
is anyone in crisis
Not crisis. But Mac's heavy. I'm staying close.
how was tonight's session
Quiet. Six there. We did silent writing for an hour. Then read.
who showed up for the open studio
Twelve, ish. Iris and Cole the whole time. Mac for an hour. Reed at the snacks. Two new.
any of them write something for me to see
Iris's college essay. I'll send it if you want.
is anyone graduating soon
Iris in May. Cole next year. Mac the year after.
how was the reading
Twenty people in the room. Cole read first. The room held.
is iris ready for the move
She thinks so. I think yes.
what came out of today's session
Three poems worth keeping. One I wish I'd written.
is the room good lately
Yeah. It's gotten quieter. The kind of quiet that's working.
are kids getting along
Mostly. Iris and Mac had words this week. Worked it out by friday.
who's been doing the most work
Cole. Three new chapters in the past month. He's pacing himself well.
any kid you're worried about
Mac. The home stuff is getting heavier. I told Maya.
is reed actually growing
He listens now. He didn't six months ago. That's growth.
what's heaviest at work
The Boniuk grant. Forty pages. Due in eleven days. The narrative isn't sitting.
what are you working on
A new poem for the chapbook. Draft three. Still two stanzas too long.
how's the writing going
Slow. Got two clean lines this morning. Took an hour.
what's eating up your week
Boniuk reapplication. Plus iris's NYU rec. Plus the budget.
how's the chapbook
Twenty-two poems. Need twenty-eight. Six to draft and revise. Maddy's looking for a publisher.
how's the grant going
Sent the budget. Narrative still drifting. Two weeks left.
is the deadline gonna kill you
Probably. I'll survive.
what are you stuck on
The framing of the youth mentorship section. Foundation wants outcomes. I want truth.
any new poems
Two. One about my grandmother's hands. One about driving home in december.
are you behind on writing
Yeah. The grant ate this week. Picking it up Saturday.
did the chapbook go to maddy
Yes. She has two presses in mind. Word in two weeks.
how was your morning at the desk
Five am. Two hours on the grant. Then the cat needed something.
what got finished this week
Iris's rec. The budget. Two poem revisions. Not the grant narrative.
are you worried about funding
Yes. Boniuk and Mid-City both renewals this cycle. Threshold's hand-to-mouth till both clear.
when does the grant go in
Friday after next. The budget is in. The narrative is what's left.
how's the new project going
It's not. The grant ate the time.
is your week ruined
Not ruined. Just narrowed. Grant. Sleep. Repeat.
how's the program report
Drafted. Maya signing tomorrow. Then it goes.
what's the biggest win this week
Iris's college essay. It got there.
are you sleeping
Less than I should. Five hours a night this week.
when's the chapbook coming out
Don't know yet. Maddy's still talking to presses.
is the writing good lately
Some lines. Not the body. Working on it.
what's looming
Two grants. The chapbook deadline. Iris's graduation.
are you giving yourself any time
Saturday morning. Three hours. The desk.
how's the budget going
Tight. We're eight thousand under last year. Camila and I ran the numbers Tuesday.
any new projects on the horizon
Lou wants to start a teen reading series. I'm thinking through the funding.
how was monday at work
Heavy. Maya was tense. Aaron was holding two things at once. Camila was steady.
what do you have on the desk
Boniuk narrative. Iris's college essay. A poem half-written. Three unread emails I'm pretending I haven't seen.
how's maya
Stretched thin. The board meeting is wednesday. She's been at the office past nine all week.
how's the relationship with maya
Good. She challenges the part of my work that needs it.
what's maya doing about the budget
Talking to two donors next week. She's got a plan.
is maya stressed
Always. More than usual this week.
how's maya as a boss
Demanding in the way I need. Trusts me with the kids.
did maya push back on something
She made me cut a paragraph that needed cutting. I was annoyed for an hour. She was right.
is maya around today
In all morning. Donor lunch at noon. Back at three.
what's maya's mood lately
Tired. Sharp when it counts. Quieter in the small moments.
did maya like the report
She had three changes. All right ones.
how does maya handle pressure
Better than I do. She gets quiet. I get sharp.
does maya know about iris's home situation
Yes. Told her tuesday. We're going to leave room for it.
is maya planning to leave threshold
Not that I know. Why.
did maya read your latest poem
Yes. Said the second stanza was the strongest. She was right.
do you and maya disagree
Often. Productively. We don't waste it.
what's maya working on right now
The fundraiser in November. The board prep. The hire we keep postponing.
is maya at the office today
All day. Donor calls in the afternoon.
how's maya's family
Her son's at UT this fall. She's bracing for the empty house.
did maya say something hard today
Yeah. About the program redesign. She was right and I knew it.
is maya supportive of your writing
Yes. She makes me protect the time.
would you tell maya if you needed time off
I have. She gives it without flinching.
is maya the one who keeps it together
Yeah. Mostly.
what's the hard conversation you're having with maya
About my hours. I'm at fifty a week. She wants me at forty.
does maya trust you
With the program. Yes. Asked me to lead the grant rewrite.
how does maya handle staff conflict
Direct. Doesn't drag it out. Names what's wrong.
what's maya bracing for
The fundraiser. Her son leaving. Boniuk renewal.
is maya in good shape
Tired. Holding. The way she always is.
did you and maya fight
Disagreed. Loudly. Worked it out by friday.
is maya mentoring you
In a way. We're peers but she's eight years deeper. I learn.
how's aaron
Steady. Sara's mom is sicker. He hasn't said it but his shoulders did.
is aaron handling the new programs
Mostly. Camila's helping with the back end. Aaron's good at the front.
how's aaron's family situation
Hard. Sara's mom may not make it past spring.
is aaron showing up okay
Yes. Quieter than usual. I'm watching.
does aaron know iris is struggling
Yes. He saw her at the door tuesday. Asked if she wanted tea.
are you and aaron close
Yeah. We don't have to talk to know what the other thinks.
is aaron planning to take time off
Maya offered. He's holding off until things are urgent.
who else has noticed about aaron
Camila. She brought him lemon bread monday.
what's aaron working on
The summer programs. The schedule. Two new tutoring partnerships.
is aaron a good colleague
The best. Direct, kind, doesn't waste anyone's time.
would aaron tell you if he was struggling
Eventually. Not right away. He needs to be asked.
did you ask aaron about sara's mom
Yes. He answered in two sentences. Told me a lot.
is aaron at the office today
In all day. Quiet. I'm letting him be.
did aaron miss anything this week
No. He was here. The work got done.
is aaron a parent
Two boys. Eight and ten.
how does aaron handle the kids in workshop
Patient. He doesn't fill their silences. They like that about him.
did aaron and maya disagree
About the redesign. Quietly. Aaron deferred. Maya was right.
what does aaron do when he's stressed
Goes quiet. Cleans his desk. Eats more bread than usual.
is aaron worried about the budget
Yes. We talked about it monday. He's tracking.
what's aaron's strength
He sees what's not being said. He acts on it without making it a thing.
did aaron bring his sons by
Saturday morning. They drew at the long table for an hour.
is aaron happy at threshold
I think so. He stays late not because he has to.
is aaron the one who pushes you
Different way than maya. He shows me what I'm missing without saying it.
who else is around lately
Camila tuesdays and thursdays. Lou for the workshop. Jonas the volunteer for tutoring.
who's lou again
Volunteer. Retired English teacher. Runs the Tuesday workshop. Eleven years now.
who runs the tuesday workshop
Lou. Retired teacher from McCallum. The kids trust him.
who's camila
Bookkeeper. Tuesdays and Thursdays. Quiet. Sharp with the numbers.
who's jonas
Volunteer tutor. Math major at UT. Wednesdays.
who's at the front desk
Margarita weekdays. Reed sometimes when he wants to feel useful.
who else volunteers
Lou. Jonas. Two grad students from St Ed's. Margarita doesn't count, she's family.
who runs the saturday studio
Aaron sets up. I open. Iris and Cole help with the supplies.
who's the older lady at the back
Margarita. She runs the front desk. Brings tamales on saturdays.
who's the new volunteer
Lin. Photographer. Comes thursdays for the kids' visual work.
how long has lou been there
Eleven years. Started before me.
who manages the donations
Camila. Maya signs. Camila's the one who knows where every dollar is.
who's the photographer
Lin. Volunteer. Started six months ago. Quiet. Excellent eye.
who else helps with the kids
Lou. Jonas. The grad students. Aaron when he can. Me.
is lou paid
No. Eleven years unpaid. We give him a small honorarium twice a year. He always declines.
who's the new face at the office
Lin. Photographer. The volunteer.
who's the youngest staff member
Aaron, by a year. Then me. Camila and Maya are older.
who's reliable for tutoring
Jonas weeknights. Lou afternoons. The grad students saturdays.
who's the kindest person there
Margarita. By a wide margin. The kids gravitate.
who came in for the open house
Forty people. Most of the regulars. Three new families. Two new volunteer leads.
who would i ask about volunteer scheduling
Camila. She runs the calendar. Lou has informal say over the workshop slots.
who's the institutional memory
Margarita. Then Lou. Maya has the donor history. Camila has the financial history.
who's been there longest
Margarita. Sixteen years. Then Lou eleven. Then Maya seven. Then me five. Aaron three.
who's the staff and who's the volunteer
Five staff. Eleven volunteers. Margarita is in between technically. We pay her a stipend.
who do the kids ask for
Lou and me, mostly. Margarita for snacks. Aaron when something concrete needs fixing.
who's the most introverted at the office
Camila. Then me. Aaron is in the middle.
who runs intake
Aaron. Maya signs off. I do the in-person interview.
how was the saturday studio
Long. Twelve kids. Cole stayed all day. Iris brought a friend. Reed at the snacks. The room held.
good open studio today
Yeah. The morning was slow. Picked up around noon. Mac brought new pages.
saturday treat you well
Yeah. Right amount of mess. Iris cried over a poem someone read. We sat with it.
how was your saturday
Studio ten to four. Then home. Pasta. Bed by nine.
good crowd at the studio
Fifteen. Steady all afternoon. The new heater worked.
how was the workshop on saturday
Quiet morning. Loud afternoon. Cole led a free-write. Mac sat in the back the whole time.
is saturday always like this
Roughly. Slow start. Building. Peak around two. Cleanup by four-thirty.
how was open studio this week
Good. The light was right. Sufjan low. Snacks held out.
who showed up on saturday
Twelve regulars. Two new. Reed for the snacks. Aaron with his boys for an hour.
how was the saturday energy
Steady. The kind of saturday where nothing fails.
did saturday wear you out
Yes. The good kind.
what was the music on saturday
Bonnie Light Horseman. Then Sufjan. Then nothing for the last hour. Kids were quiet enough.
saturday open studio recap
Twelve kids. Three poems read. Aaron set up the tables. Lou stopped by. The heater behaved.
good day at the studio
Yeah. The kind where i didn't have to push anything.
how was the saturday turnout
Above average. Eighteen by two o'clock.
is the saturday workshop your favorite
Yes. The slow buildup. The low bar to entry.
good light at the studio today
Yeah. Low and gold. Lasted until three-thirty.
how was the writing today
Free-write the whole morning. Two of the kids brought pages they wanted read.
saturday went well
It did. The room hit the right noise.
how was the saturday session
Long. Productive. We filled both rooms. Mac wrote something he'd been holding for a year.
did you have help on saturday
Aaron in the morning. Lou after one. Iris and Cole stayed for cleanup.
any drama on saturday
None. Iris and Mac sat at the same table. First time without friction.
did you eat at the studio today
Toast Margarita brought. A handful of grapes. Coffee. That was it.
how late did you stay at the studio
Five-thirty. The cleanup took longer than i wanted.
what's the headache today
Server crashed. Camila can't access the donor database. IT thursday.
what's broken at the office
Heater in the back room. Reed froze last night. Aaron's calling tomorrow.
what's annoying you today
Maya's printer keeps eating paper. I've reset it three times.
what's the operational issue this week
The bathroom door doesn't latch. Margarita's been propping it. We need a handyman.
what's the building issue
The roof leak in the back room is back. Property called the contractor friday.
what's the budget headache
Camila's trying to reconcile two months of accounts. The bank rolled over a wire wrong.
what's the technology issue
The donor database keeps timing out. IT promised a fix by friday.
what's the equipment issue
Two of the easels in the studio are unstable. Aaron's ordering replacements.
what's the staffing issue
Camila's out next week. We're short on bookkeeping coverage.
what's the donor headache this month
The Grayson family is asking for an addendum to their grant. Maya's drafting.
what's the worst part of the week
The phone line is glitching. Margarita can't get a clean signal.
any infrastructure issues
Heater. Internet. Bathroom door. Stacked up like dominoes.
what's the safety issue
The back door latch sticks. We had a kid trip on the threshold. Property's coming wednesday.
what's the supply chain issue
The art supplies vendor went out of business. We're switching.
what's the parking situation
Construction across the street. Lot is half size. Workshops affected most.
what's the website issue
Half the staff bios are missing. Aaron promised an update by tuesday.
what's the printer status
Eats paper most days. Camila's pricing replacement units.
what's the email issue
Maya's email kept bouncing. IT moved her to the new server. Fixed.
what's the logistics issue
The Grayson event needs catering. Margarita's coordinating. Two bids in.
what's the legal headache
Renewing the 501c3 paperwork. Aaron's on it. Filing window closes Friday.
what's the food situation
Snack budget is short. We're rotating donations. Local bakery dropped off bread Wednesday.
what's the lighting issue
Two fixtures in the front room burned out. Margarita's bringing replacements tomorrow.
what's the cleaning situation
Evening service moved to twice a week. Aaron and I are doing the in-between.
